title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: If x+1x=−1x + \frac{1}{x} = -1x+x1​=−1​ , then compute:x5+1x5+4x3+4x3 x^5 + \frac{1}{x^5} + 4x^3 + \frac{4}{x^3}x5+x51​+4x3+x34​​​
questions.explanation: Given: ​x+1x=−1x + \frac{1}{x} = -1x+x1​=−1​​ Concept Used: If x+1x=−1x + \frac{1}{x} = -1x+x1​=−1​, then x3=1x^3 = 1x3=1​​ Formula Used: ​x2+1x2=(x+1x)2−2x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} = \left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^2 - 2x2+x21​=(x+x1​)2−2​​ Solution: Since x3x^3x3​ = 1, then x5=x2x^5 = x^2x5=x2​ and 1x5=1x2\frac{1}{x^5} = \frac{1}{x^2}x51​=x21​​​ x2+1x2x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2}x2+x21​​ = (−1)2(-1)^2 (−1)2​- 2 = -1 Also, 4x3+4x34x^3 + \frac{4}{x^3}4x3+x34​​ = 4(1) + 4(1) = 8​​ Total Value = -1 + 8 = 7 So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A conical vessel is cut at one-fourth its height from the top, parallel to its base. What is the ratio of the volume of the top part to the bottom frustum part?
questions.explanation: Given: ​h=14Hh = \frac{1}{4}Hh=41​H​​ Concept Used: Ratio of volumes of similar cones = (Ratio of heights)³ Formula Used: ​V1V2=(h1h2)3\frac{V1}{V2} = \left(\frac{h1}{h2}\right)^3V2V1​=(h2h1​)3​​ Solution: Ratio of small cone to big cone = (14)3\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^3(41​)3​ = 164\frac{1}{64}641​​​ Bottom part (frustum) = 64 - 1 = 63. Ratio = 1 : 63. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A sector of a circle has a central angle of 60° and a radius of 12 cm. Another sector of the same circle has a central angle of π/2\pi/2 π/2 radians. What is the ratio of the area of the first sector to the area of the second sector?
questions.explanation: Given: Central angle of first sector = 60° Central angle of second sector = π/2 radians Radius = 12 cm Formula used: Area of a sector = (θ / 2) × r² where θ is in radians Solution: Convert 60° into radians 60° = π/3 Now apply formula Area₁ = ( (π/3) / 2 ) × 12² Area₂ = ( (π/2) / 2 ) × 12² Taking ratio, common factors 12² and ½ cancel Area₁ : Area₂ = (π/3) : (π/2) = ⅓ : ½ = 2 : 3 Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Two right circular cylinders have the same diagonal length of 25 cm. The difference between their radii is 2 cm. If the difference between the squares of their heights is 176 cm², then what is the difference between their Curved Surface Areas (CSA)?

In a circular race of 1000 m, Ravi and Sumit start from the same point and at the same time, running at speeds of 9 km/hr and 27 km/hr, respectively. When will they meet again for the first time on the track if they are running in the same direction?
questions.explanation: Given Distance = 1000 m = 1 km Speeds: 9 km/hr and 27 km/hr (Same direction) Formula Used Time = DistanceRelative Speed \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Relative Speed}}Relative SpeedDistance​​​ Relative Speed (Same Direction) = S1 - S2 Solution Relative Speed = 27 - 9 = 18 km/hr Convert to m/s: 18×518=5 m/s18 \times \frac{5}{18} = 5 \text{ m/s}18×185​=5 m/s​​ Time = 10005=200 seconds \frac{1000}{5} = 200 \text{ seconds}51000​=200 seconds​​ Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A company's stock price increases by x% in the first quarter and decreases by the same x% in the second quarter. If the final stock price is 14% lower than the initial price, what is the value of x? (Round off the answer to two decimal places.)
questions.explanation: Given First change = +x% Second change = -x% Net result = -14% (decrease) Formula Used Net percentage change = A + B + A×B100 \frac{A \times B}{100}100A×B​​​ Solution Here A = x and B = -x. Net Change = x - x - x2100\frac{x^2}{100}100x2​​​ ​−14=−x2100 14=x2100 x2=1400 x=1400 x=1014 Using14≈3.7416: x≈10×3.7416=37.416 -14 = -\frac{x^2}{100} \\\ \\14 = \frac{x^2}{100} \\\ \\x^2 = 1400 \\\ \\x = \sqrt{1400} \\\ \\x = 10\sqrt{14} \\\ \\Using \sqrt{14} \approx 3.7416: \\\ \\x \approx 10 \times 3.7416 = 37.416 \\\ \\−14=−100x2​ 14=100x2​ x2=1400 x=1400​ x=1014​ Using14​≈3.7416: x≈10×3.7416=37.416 ​​ Rounding to two decimal places: 37.42 Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: The capacity of a cylindrical vessel is 2,94,375 cm³. If its base diameter is 50 cm, then what is the height (in cm) of the cylindrical vessel? (Use π = 3.14.)
questions.explanation: Given Volume of vessel (V) = 2,94,375 cm³ Diameter = 50 cm Radius (r) = 25 cm π = 3.14 Formula Used Volume of cylinder = πr2h \pi r^2 hπr2h​​ Solution Substituting the values in the formula: ​294375=3.14×(25)2×h 294375=3.14×625×h 294375=1962.5×h h=2943751962.5 h=150 cm 294375 = 3.14 \times (25)^2 \times h \\\ \\294375 = 3.14 \times 625 \times h \\\ \\294375 = 1962.5 \times h \\\ \\h = \frac{294375}{1962.5} \\\ \\h = 150 \text{ cm} \\\ \\294375=3.14×(25)2×h 294375=3.14×625×h 294375=1962.5×h h=1962.5294375​ h=150 cm ​​ Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A pipe can fill a tank in 8 hours. Another pipe can empty the filled tank in 40 hours. If both the pipes are opened simultaneously, then the time (in hours) in which the tank will be half filled, is:
questions.explanation: Given Pipe A (Fill) = 8 hours Pipe B (Empty) = 40 hours Formula Used Total Efficiency = 1A−1B\frac{1}{A} - \frac{1}{B}A1​−B1​​​ Solution Efficiency of Pipe A = 18 \frac{1}{8}81​​​ Efficiency of Pipe B = 140\frac{1}{40}401​​ Combined Efficiency = 18−140=5−140=440=110 \frac{1}{8} - \frac{1}{40} = \frac{5 - 1}{40} = \frac{4}{40} = \frac{1}{10}81​−401​=405−1​=404​=101​​​ Time to fill the full tank = 10 hours. Time to fill half the tank = 102=5 hours.\frac{10}{2} = 5 \text{ hours}.210​=5 hours.​​ Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Two workers, A and B, working together can complete a task in 14 days. A alone can complete the same task in 24 days. They start the work together, but after 5 days, A falls sick and B completes the remaining work alone. In how many total days was the work completed?
questions.explanation: Given Time (A + B) = 14 days Time (A) = 24 days Solution Let Total Work = LCM(14, 24) = 168 units. Efficiency (A + B) = 168 / 14 = 12 units/day Efficiency (A) = 168 / 24 = 7 units/day Efficiency (B) = 12 - 7 = 5 units/day Work done in first 5 days (A + B) = 12 ×5 \times 5×5​ = 60 units. Remaining work = 168 - 60 = 108 units. Time for B to finish remaining work = 108 / 5 = 21.6 days. Total Time = 5 + 21.6 = 26.6 days. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: The cost price of an article is ₹420, and its marked price is ₹1,120. If the seller gives a discount of 55% on the marked price, what will be his profit percentage?
If (a + b):(a - b) = 7:3 and (x + y):(x - y) = 8:5, find the ratio of a/x : b/y.
questions.explanation: Given ​a+ba−b=73 x+yx−y=85\frac{a+b}{a-b} = \frac{7}{3} \\\ \\\frac{x+y}{x-y} = \frac{8}{5}a−ba+b​=37​ x−yx+y​=58​​​ Formula Used Componendo and Dividendo Rule: ​If AB=CD,thenA+BA−B=C+DC−D\text{If} \, \frac{A}{B} = \frac{C}{D}, \text {then} \frac{A+B}{A-B} = \frac{C+D}{C-D}IfBA​=DC​,thenA−BA+B​=C−DC+D​​​ Solution ​(a+b)+(a−b)(a+b)−(a−b)=7+37−3 2a2b=104 ab=52 (x+y)+(x−y)(x+y)−(x−y)=8+58−5 2x2y=133 xy=133 ⟹ yx=313 a/xb/y=ax×yb=(ab)×(yx) =52×313=1526 \frac{(a+b)+(a-b)}{(a+b)-(a-b)} = \frac{7+3}{7-3} \\\ \\\frac{2a}{2b} = \frac{10}{4}\\\ \\\frac{a}{b} = \frac{5}{2}\\\ \\\frac{(x+y)+(x-y)}{(x+y)-(x-y)} = \frac{8+5}{8-5}\\\ \\\frac{2x}{2y} = \frac{13}{3}\\\ \\\frac{x}{y} = \frac{13}{3} \implies \frac{y}{x} = \frac{3}{13}\\\ \\\frac{a/x}{b/y} = \frac{a}{x} \times \frac{y}{b} = (\frac{a}{b}) \times (\frac{y}{x})\\\ \\= \frac{5}{2} \times \frac{3}{13} = \frac{15}{26}\\\ \\(a+b)−(a−b)(a+b)+(a−b)​=7−37+3​ 2b2a​=410​ ba​=25​ (x+y)−(x−y)(x+y)+(x−y)​=8−58+5​ 2y2x​=313​ yx​=313​⟹xy​=133​ b/ya/x​=xa​×by​=(ba​)×(xy​) =25​×133​=2615​ ​​ Ratio is 15 : 26 Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Praveen and Hemant start a business together. Praveen invests ₹83,000 more than Hemant for 7 months, while Hemant invests for 3 months. Out of a total profit of ₹5,342, Praveen’s share exceeds Hemant’s by ₹2,671. Find the amount invested by Praveen.
questions.explanation: Given Total Profit = 5342 Praveen's Share - Hemant's Share = 2671 Time: Praveen = 7 months, Hemant = 3 months Praveen's Investment = Hemant's Investment + 83000 Formula Used Ratio of Profit = Ratio of (Investment × \times ×​Time) Solution Step 1: Find Profit Shares P + H = 5342 P - H = 2671 Adding equations: 2P = 8013 ⟹ \implies⟹​ P = 4006.5 Subtracting: 2H = 2671 ⟹ \implies⟹​ H = 1335.5 Ratio P : H = 4006.51335.5=31 \frac{4006.5}{1335.5} = \frac{3}{1}1335.54006.5​=13​​​ Let Hemant's Inv = I, Praveen's Inv = I + 83000 ​(I+83000)×7I×3=31 \frac{(I + 83000) \times 7}{I \times 3} = \frac{3}{1} \\\ \\I×3(I+83000)×7​=13​ 7(I + 83000) = 9I 7I + 581000 = 9I 2I = 581000 I = 290500 Praveen's Investment = 290500 + 83000 = 373500 Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: A man invests ₹20,000 in three schemes in the ratio 2:3:5. The schemes offer returns of 5%, 10%, and 15% respectively. What is the total income generated?
questions.explanation: Given: Total Investment = ₹20,000. Ratio = 2:3:5. Sum of ratios = 10. Solution: Investment in Scheme 1 =210×20000 \frac{2}{10} \times 20000102​×20000​ = 4000. Investment in Scheme 2 =310×20000 \frac{3}{10} \times 20000103​×20000​ = 6000. Investment in Scheme 3 = 510×20000\frac{5}{10} \times 20000105​×20000​ = 10000. Income: From 1: 5% of 4000 = 200. From 2: 10% of 6000 = 600. From 3: 15% of 10000 = 1500. Total Income = 200 + 600 + 1500 = ₹2300. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: A shopkeeper marks his goods to gain 60% on the cost price but allows a discount of 15% on the marked price. What is his actual profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Given: Markup % = 60%. Discount % = 15%. Formula Used: Successive percentage change: A + B + AB100\frac{AB}{100}100AB​​​ Here A = +60, B = -15. Solution: Profit % = 60 - 15 - 60×15100\frac{60 \times 15}{100}10060×15​​​ = 45 - 900100\frac{900}{100}100900​​​ = 45 - 9 = 36%. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: In what ratio must water be mixed with milk costing ₹60 per litre so as to gain 20% by selling the mixture at ₹54 per litre?
questions.explanation: Given: Cost of Milk = ₹60/L. Cost of Water = ₹0/L. Selling Price (SP) of Mixture = ₹54/L. Gain = 20%. Formula Used: Cost Price (CP) = SP×100100+Gain%\frac{SP \times 100}{100 + Gain\%}100+Gain%SP×100​​​ Rule of Alligation. Solution: Find CP of Mixture: CP = 54×100120=54012\frac{54 \times 100}{120} = \frac{540}{12}12054×100​=12540​​ = ₹45. Apply Alligation: Water (0) : Milk (60) Mean Price (45) Difference for Water: 60 - 45 = 15. Difference for Milk: 45 - 0 = 45. Ratio = 15 : 45 = 1 : 3. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: A person invested ₹15,000 in two different schemes. He invested one part for 5 years at 4% simple interest and the other part for the same duration at 6% simple interest. If the interest earned from the second part was ₹1,500 more than the interest earned from the first part, how much money was invested in the second scheme?
questions.explanation: Given: Total investment = ₹15,000 Scheme 1: Time = 5 years, Rate = 4% Scheme 2: Time = 5 years, Rate = 6% Difference in interest = ₹1,500 Formula Used: ​SI=P×R×T100 SI = \frac{P \times R \times T}{100}SI=100P×R×T​​​ Solution: Let the amount invested in the second scheme be x. Then, the amount invested in the first scheme is 15000 - x. Interest from second scheme: SI2=x×6×5100=30x100SI_2 = \frac{x \times 6 \times 5}{100} = \frac{30x}{100}SI2​=100x×6×5​=10030x​ ​​Interest from first scheme: SI1=(15000−x)×4×5100=20(15000−x)100 SI_1 = \frac{(15000 - x) \times 4 \times 5}{100} = \frac{20(15000 - x)}{100}SI1​=100(15000−x)×4×5​=10020(15000−x)​ ​According to the problem: SI2−SI1SI_2 - SI_1SI2​−SI1​​ = 1500 ​30x100−20(15000−x)100=1500 \frac{30x}{100} - \frac{20(15000 - x)}{100} = 150010030x​−10020(15000−x)​=1500​​ 30x - 300000 + 20x = 150000 50x = 450000 x = 9000 The amount invested in the second scheme is ₹9,000. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: △PQR has sides PQ = 8 cm, QR = 10 cm, PR = 12 cm. △LMN is congruent to △PQR. What is the perimeter of △LMN?
questions.explanation: Given Sides of△ \triangle △​PQR: PQ = 8 cm, QR = 10 cm, PR = 12 cm ​△LMN≅△PQR\triangle LMN \cong \triangle PQR△LMN≅△PQR​​ Formula Used 1. Perimeter = Sum of all sides 2. Congruent triangles have equal corresponding sides and perimeter. Solution Perimeter of △\triangle△​ PQR = 8 + 10 + 12 = 30 cm Since △\triangle△​ LMN is congruent to △\triangle△​ PQR, their perimeters must be identical. Perimeter of △ \triangle△​ LMN = 30 cm Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: If cos⁡A = ⅘ and sin⁡B = 5/13 , A and B∈(0,π2) , find cos(A - B).
questions.explanation: Given: cosA = ⅘ sinB = 5/13 A, B ∈ (0, π/2) Find cos(A − B) Concept Used: Trigonometric identity of cosine difference and Pythagoras identity. Formula Used: cos(A − B) = cosA cosB + sinA sinB sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 Solution: ​cos⁡A=45 sin⁡A=1−cos⁡2A =1−1625 =925 =35 sin⁡B=513 cos⁡B=1−sin⁡2B =1−25169 =144169 =1213 cos⁡(A−B)=cos⁡Acos⁡B+sin⁡Asin⁡B =(45×1213)+(35×513) =4865+1565=6365\cos A = \frac{4}{5}\\ \ \\\sin A = \sqrt{1 - \cos^2 A}\\ \ \\= \sqrt{1 - \frac{16}{25}}\\ \ \\ = \sqrt{\frac{9}{25}}\\ \ \\= \frac{3}{5}\\ \ \\\sin B = \frac{5}{13}\\ \ \\\cos B = \sqrt{1 - \sin^2 B}\\ \ \\= \sqrt{1 - \frac{25}{169}}\\ \ \\= \sqrt{\frac{144}{169}}\\ \ \\= \frac{12}{13}\\ \ \\\cos(A - B) = \cos A \cos B + \sin A \sin B\\ \ \\= \left(\frac{4}{5} \times \frac{12}{13}\right) + \left(\frac{3}{5} \times \frac{5}{13}\right)\\ \ \\= \frac{48}{65} + \frac{15}{65}= \frac{63}{65}cosA=54​ sinA=1−cos2A​ =1−2516​​ =259​​ =53​ sinB=135​ cosB=1−sin2B​ =1−16925​​ =169144​​ =1312​ cos(A−B)=cosAcosB+sinAsinB =(54​×1312​)+(53​×135​) =6548​+6515​=6563​ ​​

questions.question: If a = 5\sqrt{5}5​​ and b = 5 -5 \sqrt{5}5​​, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: Given: a = 5 \sqrt{5}5​​​ b = 5 - 5\sqrt{5}5​​​ Solution: Calculate a + b: a + b =5+(5−5) \sqrt{5} + (5 - \sqrt{5})5​+(5−5​)​ = 5. Since 5 is a rational number, a + b is rational. Check other options: a - b =5−(5−5)=25−5 \sqrt{5} - (5 - \sqrt{5}) = 2\sqrt{5} - 55​−(5−5​)=25​−5​ (Irrational). ab =5(5−5)=55−5 \sqrt{5}(5 - \sqrt{5}) = 5\sqrt{5} - 55​(5−5​)=55​−5​ (Irrational). Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: A man invests ₹10,000 at 10% compound interest per annum. After 1 year, he adds ₹5,000 more. What will be the total amount at the end of 2 years from the start?
questions.explanation: Given: Initial P = 10,000. Rate = 10%. After 1 year, adds 5,000. Solution: Amount after 1 year = 10,000 × 1.10 = 11,000. New Principal for 2nd year = 11,000 + 5,000 = 16,000. Interest for 2nd year = 16,000 × 10% = 1,600. Total Amount at end of year 2 = 16,000 + 1,600 = 17,600. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Working 6 hours a day, Raman can complete a piece of work in 15 days and working 8 hours a day Mohan can complete the same work in 10 days. In how many days would they complete the work, if working together 10 hours in a day?
questions.explanation: Given Raman's total hours = 15×6=90 15 \times 6 = 9015×6=90​ hours Mohan's total hours = 10×8=8010 \times 8 = 8010×8=80​ hours Combined working hours per day = 10 hours Formula Used Time taken together = A×BA+B \frac{A \times B}{A + B}A+BA×B​​​ Solution Total hours needed together =90×8090+80=7200170=72017 \frac{90 \times 80}{90 + 80} = \frac{7200}{170} = \frac{720}{17} 90+8090×80​=1707200​=17720​​hours Working 10 hours a day, number of days: Days = 720/1710=7217\frac{720/17}{10} = \frac{72}{17}10720/17​=1772​​​ Days =4417 4 \frac{4}{17}4174​​days Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Rohan in his football career, scored 8 goals on an average in 10 matches. If he scored 12 goals on an average in the first 5 matches and 6 goals on an average in the last 3 matches, then find the average of the goals scored by him per match in the remaining 2 matches.
questions.explanation: Given Average goals in 10 matches = 8 Average goals in first 5 matches = 12 Average goals in last 3 matches = 6 Formula Used Total = Average × Number of items Remaining Average = Remaining TotalRemaining Matches\frac{\text{Remaining Total}}{\text{Remaining Matches}}Remaining MatchesRemaining Total​​​ Solution Total goals in 10 matches = 10×8=8010 \times 8 = 8010×8=80​​ Total goals in first 5 matches = 5×12=605 \times 12 = 605×12=60​ Total goals in last 3 matches = 3×6=183 \times 6 = 183×6=18​​ Total goals in 8 matches = 60 + 18 = 78 Goals in remaining 2 matches = 80 - 78 = 2 Average of remaining 2 matches = 22\frac{2}{2}22​​ = 1goal Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Assertion (A): Two circles touching each other externally have exactly three common tangents. Reason (R): One direct common tangent and two transverse common tangents can be drawn.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
questions.explanation: Given: Assertion (A): Two circles touching externally have 3 common tangents. Reason (R): One direct and two transverse tangents. Solution: When two circles touch each other externally: 1. They have two direct common tangents. 2. They have one transverse common tangent passing through the point of contact. Total common tangents = 3. So, Assertion (A) is True. Reason (R) states "One direct and two transverse", which is incorrect. It should be "Two direct and one transverse". So, Reason (R) is False. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: The bar graph shows the sales of cell phones (in thousands) from four branches of a company during two consecutive years 2018 and 2019. What is the average sales of all the branches (in thousands) for the year 2018? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/ceda5630-7ec7-4e1c-af0c-eef56abbb041.png)
questions.explanation: ​Given: The sales data for 4 branches in year 2018 from the graph. Solution: Sum of sales for all 4 branches in 2018=60 (in thousands). Average Sales=Sum of SalesNumber of Branches Average Sales=604=15. Final Answer 15\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{The sales data for 4 branches in year 2018 from the graph.} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Sum of sales for all 4 branches in 2018} = 60 \text{ (in thousands)}. \\ \space \, \\ \text{Average Sales} = \frac{\text{Sum of Sales}}{\text{Number of Branches}} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Average Sales} = \frac{60}{4} = 15. \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 15Given: The sales data for 4 branches in year 2018 from the graph. Solution: Sum of sales for all 4 branches in 2018=60 (in thousands). Average Sales=Number of BranchesSum of Sales​ Average Sales=460​=15. Final Answer 15​​
